ICAPS-2022 Schedule Overview

June 21 June 22 June 23 June 24
6Opening (start: +30min)
7Invited Talk: Malte Helmert(9a) Search(9b) Multi-Agent Planning(18a) Classical Planning(18b) RoboticsInvited Talk: Tim Miller
8Social: Get TogetherSocial: Get TogetherSocial: GamesSocial: Open Discussion
9(1a) Planning Representations and Scheduling(1b) Verification(10a) Goal and Plan Recognition(10b) Scheduling Applications(19a) Expressivity and Complexity(19b) Path Finding(27a) Reformulation(27b) Probabilistic Planning
10(2a) Reinforcement Learning(2b) Heuristics in Classical Planning(11a) Heuristics in Classical and Multi-Objective Planning(11b) Applications(20a) Explainable Planning and Scheduling(20b) Heuristics and Heuristic Search(28a) Model-Based Reasoning in Classical Planning(28b) Temporal Planning
11Poster and Demo SessionCompetition PanelPoster and Demo SessionPoster and Demo Session
12
13(3a) Model-Based Reasoning in Classical Planning(3b) Learning in Scheduling Applications(12a) Numeric Planning(12b) Deep LearningIndustry Panel(29a) Multi-Agent and Tree Search(29b) Motion Planning and Deep RL
14(4a) Search(4b) Optimal Planning(13a) Epistemic Planning(13b) Heuristics in Classical Planning(21a) Applications(21b) Temporal Planning and Reinforcement LearningCommunity Meeting
(ends +20min)
15Career Development PanelSocial: Pseudonym Get TogetherSocial: Puzzle Game Team Challenge
16(5a) Problem Reformulation(5b) Heuristics and Heuristic SearchInvited Talk: Emma Brunskill(22a) Reinforcement Learning(22b) Robotics(30a) Scheduling
(start: +20 min)		(30b) Heuristics in Classical Planning
(start: +20 min)
17(6a) Multi-Agent Path Finding(6b) Deep Learning(14a) Search(14b) Verification(23a) Abstraction Heuristics(23b) Reinforcement Learning(31a) User Interfaces for Explainable Planning
(start: +20 min)		(31b) Scheduling
(start: +20 min)
18Poster and Demo SessionPoster and Demo SessionInvited Talk: Sidd Srinivasa
19Closing and Final Get Together
20(7a) Probabilistic Planning(7b) Multi-Agent Planning and Scheduling(15a) Planning Decompositions and Learning(15b) Explainable Planning and Scheduling(24a) Reinforcement Learning in Applications(24b) User Interactions and Top-Quality Planning
21Social: Speed GatheringSocial: Pseudonym Get TogetherSocial: Get Together
22(8a) Hierarchical Task Networks(8b) Rewards in Reinforcement Learning(16a) Multi-Agent Planning(16b) Reinforcement Learning(25a) Heuristic Search(25b) Epistemic Planning
23Invited Talk: Sven Koenig(17a) Applications(17b) Path and Motion Planning(26a) Heuristics(26b) Scheduling
24Social: GamesPoster and Demo SessionPoster and Demo Session

Detailed Schedule

Tuesday 21

6:00 UTCOpening (start: +30min)
7:00 UTCInvited Talk: Malte Helmert
8:00 UTCSocial: Get Together
9:00 UTC (1a) Planning Representations and Scheduling
Session Chair: Neil Yorke-Smith		 (1b) Verification
Session Chair: Erez Karpas
Generalized Linear Integer Numeric Planning (Main Track)
Xiaoyou Lin, Qingliang Chen, Liangda Fang, Quanlong Guan, Weiqi Luo and Kaile Su 		Neural Network Action Policy Verification via Predicate Abstraction (Main Track)
Marcel Vinzent, Marcel Steinmetz and Jörg Hoffmann
Uniform Machine Scheduling with Predictions (Main Track)
Tianming Zhao, Wei Li and Albert Zomaya 		Debugging a Policy: Automatic Action-Policy Testing in AI Planning (Main Track)
Marcel Steinmetz, Daniel Fišer, Hasan Ferit Enişer, Patrick Ferber, Timo Gros, Philippe Heim, Daniel Höller, Xandra Schuler, Valentin Wüstholz, Maria Christakis and Joerg Hoffmann
Learning Sketches for Decomposing Planning Problems into Subproblems of Bounded Width (Main Track)
Dominik Drexler, Jendrik Seipp and Hector Geffner 		Decidability and Complexity of Action-Based Temporal Planning over Dense Time (Journal Presentations Track)
Nicola Gigante, Andrea Micheli, Angelo Montanari and Enrico Scala
10:00 UTC (2a) Reinforcement Learning
Session Chair: Vaneet Aggarwal		 (2b) Heuristics in Classical Planning
Session Chair: Jörg Hoffmann
Deep reinforcement learning for a multi-objective online order batching problem (Industry and Applications Track)
Martijn Beeks, Reza Refaei Afshar, Yingqian Zhang, Remco Dijkman, Claudy van Dorst and Stijn de Looijer 		Efficient Computation and Informative Estimation of h+ by Integer and Linear Programming (Main Track)
Masood Feyzbakhsh Rankooh and Jussi Rintanen
Reinforcement Learning Approach to Solve Dynamic Bi-Objective Police Patrol Dispatching and Rescheduling Problem (Industry and Applications Track)
Waldy Joe, Hoong Chuin Lau and Jonathan Pan 		Detecting Unsolvability Based on Separating Functions (Main Track)
Remo Christen, Salomé Eriksson, Florian Pommerening and Malte Helmert
Learning Multi-agent Action Coordination via Electing First-move Agent (Planning and Learning Track)
Jingqing Ruan, Linghui Meng, Xuantang Xiong, Dengpeng Xing and Bo Xu 		New Refinement Strategies for Cartesian Abstractions (Main Track)
David Speck and Jendrik Seipp
11:00 UTCPoster and Demo Session
13:00 UTC (3a) Model-Based Reasoning in Classical Planning
Session Chair: Michael Katz		 (3b) Learning in Scheduling Applications
Session Chair: Nysret Musliu
Loopless Top-k Planning (Main Track)
Julian von Tschammer, Robert Mattmüller and David Speck 		Stochastic Resource Optimization over Heterogeneous Graph Neural Networks for Failure-Predictive Maintenance Scheduling (Industry and Applications Track)

Encoding Lifted Classical Planning in Propositional Logic (Main Track)
Daniel Höller and Gregor Behnke 		OFFICERS: Operational Framework For Intelligent Crime-and-Emergency Response Scheduling (Industry and Applications Track)
Jonathan Chase, Siong Thye Goh, Phong Tran and Hoong Chuin Lau
Classical Planning as QBF without Grounding (Main Track)
Irfansha Shaik and Jaco van de Pol 		Reinforcement Learning of Dispatching Strategies for Large-Scale Industrial Scheduling (Planning and Learning Track)

Session Chair: Michael Katz		 (4b) Optimal Planning
Session Chair: Jörg Hoffmann
Beam Search: Faster and Monotonic (Main Track)
Sofia Lemons, Carlos Linares, Robert Holte and Wheeler Ruml 		Optimal Mixed Strategies for Cost-Adversarial Planning Games (Main Track)

Best-First Width Search for Lifted Classical Planning (Main Track)
Augusto B. Corrêa and Jendrik Seipp 		It Costs to Get Costs! A Heuristic-Based Scalable Goal Assignment Algorithm for Multi-Robot Systems (Main Track)

Beyond Stars - Generalized Topologies for Decoupled Search (Main Track)
Daniel Gnad, Álvaro Torralba and Daniel Fišer 		Optimising the Stability in Plan Repair via Compilation (Main Track)
Alessandro Saetti and Enrico Scala

16:00 UTC (5a) Problem Reformulation
Session Chair: Sunandita Patra		 (5b) Heuristics and Heuristic Search

19:00 UTCClosing and Final Get Together

ICAPS is the premier forum for exchanging news and research results on theory and applications of intelligent planning and scheduling technology.

© 2022 ICAPS
